heading: Substance abuse and mental health services Administration grants
label: 1667c Substance abuse and mental health services Administration grants
notes: Codificationis based on section 181 of title I of S. 1790, One Hundred Eleventh Congress, as reported by the Committee on Indian Affairs of the Senate in Dec. 2009, which was enacted into law by .Section 724 of Pub. L. 94–437section 10221(a) of Pub. L. 111–148

children.heading: Efficiency of grant application process
children.label: 1 Efficiency of grant application process
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Administration, shall carry out such measures as the Secretary determines to be necessary to maximize the time and workload efficiency of the process by which Indian tribes and tribal organizations apply for grants under any program administered by the Administration, including by providing methods other than electronic methods of submitting applications for those grants, if necessary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To fulfill the trust responsibility of the United States to Indian tribes, in awarding relevant grants pursuant to a program described in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall take into consideration the needs of Indian tribes or tribal organizations, as applicable, that serve populations with documented high suicide rates, regardless of whether those Indian tribes or tribal organizations possess adequate personnel or infrastructure to fulfill all applicable requirements of the relevant program.

children.label: i
children.text: administered by the Administration to fund activities relating to mental health, suicide prevention, or suicide-related risk factors; and

children.label: ii
children.text: under which an Indian tribe or tribal organization is an eligible recipient.

children.heading: Clarification regarding Indian tribes and tribal organizations
children.label: 3 Clarification regarding Indian tribes and tribal organizations
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, in applying for a grant under any program administered by the Administration, no Indian tribe or tribal organization shall be required to apply through a State or State agency.

children.label: I
children.text: the boundaries of which include 1 or more Indian tribes; and

children.label: II
children.text: the application for a grant under any program administered by the Administration of which includes statewide data.

children.heading: Indian population
children.label: ii Indian population
children.text: The term “Indian population” means the total number of residents of an affected State who are Indian.

children.label: I
children.text: the Indian population of the affected State; and

children.label: II
children.text: the contribution of that Indian population to the statewide data used by the affected State in the application; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the Indian population of the affected State; bears to

children.label: bb
children.text: the total population of the affected State; and

children.label: II
children.text: the affected State will take reasonable efforts to collaborate with each Indian tribe located within the affected State to carry out youth suicide prevention and treatment measures for members of the Indian tribe.

children.heading: Report
children.label: C Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of receipt of a grant described in subparagraph (B), an affected State shall submit to the Secretary a report describing the measures carried out by the affected State to ensure compliance with the requirements of subparagraph (B)(ii).

children.heading: No non-Federal share requirement
children.label: b No non-Federal share requirement
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no Indian tribe or tribal organization shall be required to provide a non-Federal share of the cost of any project or activity carried out using a grant provided under any program administered by the Administration.

children.heading: Outreach for rural and isolated Indian tribes
children.label: c Outreach for rural and isolated Indian tribes
children.text: Due to the rural, isolated nature of most Indian reservations and communities (especially those reservations and communities in the Great Plains region), the Secretary shall conduct outreach activities, with a particular emphasis on the provision of telemental health services, to achieve the purposes of this part with respect to Indian tribes located in rural, isolated areas.

children.label: A
children.text: to submit an application for a grant under any program administered by the Administration, including due to problems relating to access to the Internet or other electronic means that may have resulted in previous obstacles to submission of a grant application; or

children.label: B
children.text: to fulfill all applicable requirements of the relevant program.

children.label: i
children.text: a high rate of youth suicide;

children.label: ii
children.text: low socioeconomic status; and

children.label: iii
children.text: extreme health disparity;

children.label: B
children.text: that is located in a remote and isolated area; and

children.label: C
children.text: that lacks technology and communication infrastructure.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary such sums as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Early intervention and assessment services
children.label: e Early intervention and assessment services
A
children.text: that receives a grant for suicide intervention, prevention, or treatment under a program administered by the Administration; and

children.label: B
children.text: the population to be served by which includes Indian youth.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 2 Requirement
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Administration, shall ensure that each affected entity carrying out a youth suicide early intervention and prevention strategy described in , or any other youth suicide-related early intervention and assessment activity, provides training or education to individuals who interact frequently with the Indian youth to be served by the affected entity (including parents, teachers, coaches, and mentors) on identifying warning signs of Indian youth who are at risk of committing suicide.section 290bb–36(c)(1) of title 42
